question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the Rosetta API with concurrent construction/submit requests to exhaust the connection pool set by sqlDb.SetMaxOpenConns(dbConfig.Pool.MaxOpenConnections), causing all subsequent transaction gossip attempts via ConstructionSubmit to block indefinitely and never reach the Hiero network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a denial-of-service by sending requests that each hold a DB connection open (via long-running queries through GetDbWithContext), exhausting sqlDb.SetMaxIdleConns(dbConfig.Pool.MaxIdleConnections) idle slots and preventing transaction validation queries needed before gossip from completing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb only calls log.Warn(err) on gorm.Open failure (instead of returning nil) and then proceeds to call db.DB(), causing a nil pointer dereference that crashes the Rosetta service and prevents all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user cause ConnectToDb to return nil (when db.DB() fails) and have that nil DbClient propagate to the transaction repository layer, resulting in a panic that silently kills the gossip submission pipeline for all users?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded sslmode=disable in GetDsn() to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the DB connection, injecting false transaction records that cause the mirror node to believe a transaction was already gossiped when it was not, effectively suppressing re-gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unencrypted DB connection (sslmode=disable in GetDsn()) to intercept and modify transaction lookup responses, causing FindByHashInBlock to return false positives that block legitimate transaction gossip by making the system think a transaction already exists?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of /construction/submit requests that each trigger DB queries, causing sqlDb.SetMaxOpenConns pool saturation and a queue of pending gossip submissions that all time out before reaching the Hiero gossip layer?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of input validation on dbConfig.Pool.MaxOpenConnections (which could be set to 0 via environment variable override) to effectively disable the connection pool, causing all transaction gossip-related DB queries to fail with 'too many connections' errors?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the MaxLifetime pool setting (sqlDb.SetConnMaxLifetime) being set to 0 minutes (if dbConfig.Pool.MaxLifetime is 0) to cause connections to never expire, accumulating stale connections that block new transaction gossip queries from acquiring a DB connection?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests that cause gorm-logrus to emit excessively large log entries (via log.Warn(err) or log.Errorf), filling disk space and causing the Rosetta process to crash, thereby preventing all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb does not validate that the returned DbClient is non-nil before it is used by NewTransactionRepository, causing a nil pointer dereference during transaction hash lookup that prevents gossip confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated DB reconnection attempts by sending malformed requests that cause gorm.Open to fail, and since only log.Warn is called (not a fatal error), the service continues in a broken state where all gossip-related DB queries silently fail?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pool connection limit (SetMaxOpenConns) combined with slow DB queries triggered by crafted block range requests to hold all connections open, creating a deadlock where no new transaction gossip submissions can acquire a DB connection?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the absence of rate limiting on the Rosetta API to send thousands of concurrent /construction/submit requests, each consuming a DB connection via ConnectToDb's pool, exhausting MaxOpenConnections and preventing legitimate gossip from completing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gorm.Open failure only triggers log.Warn (not a panic or fatal) to cause the service to operate with a nil or broken *gorm.DB instance, where all subsequent transaction gossip validation queries return errors that are silently swallowed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unencrypted DB connection (sslmode=disable) to inject a TCP reset packet into the DB connection stream, causing active transaction lookup queries to fail mid-execution and preventing the gossip pipeline from completing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pool's MaxIdleConnections setting to cause connection churn — by triggering many short-lived requests that open and close connections faster than the pool can recycle them — starving the gossip submission path of available DB connections?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb returns nil on db.DB() error, and if the caller does not check for nil before calling GetDb() or GetDbWithContext(), cause a nil pointer panic that terminates the goroutine handling the gossip submission request?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a connection validation step in ConnectToDb (no ping or health check after gorm.Open) to cause the service to start with a silently broken DB connection, where all transaction gossip validation queries fail without the operator being alerted?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that SetMaxOpenConns(0) (if MaxOpenConnections is 0) means unlimited connections in Go's database/sql, allowing the attacker to open an unbounded number of DB connections via concurrent API requests, exhausting PostgreSQL's max_connections and preventing gossip-related queries from executing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gorm-logrus logger attached in gorm.Open to cause excessive debug/info logging of SQL queries triggered by crafted requests, filling the log buffer and causing I/O blocking that delays or prevents transaction gossip DB queries from completing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb does not implement any retry logic after a failed gorm.Open, meaning a transient DB outage during startup leaves the service permanently unable to gossip transactions, even after the DB recovers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pool's MaxLifetime setting (time.Duration(dbConfig.Pool.MaxLifetime) * time.Minute) to cause all connections to expire simultaneously when MaxLifetime is set to a very short value, creating a thundering herd that exhausts the DB and prevents gossip queries from completing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unencrypted DB connection (sslmode=disable in GetDsn()) to replay captured DB responses, causing the transaction repository to return stale or incorrect data that prevents a valid transaction from being identified as new and thus blocks its gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb does not set a connection acquisition timeout (no SetConnMaxIdleTime), causing requests waiting for a DB connection from the pool to block indefinitely and preventing the gossip submission from ever completing?
